stage_prompt.name: Refactor Fleet Tools for Multi-Project Seam

comprehension_gate.rule: MANDATORY FIRST ACTION. Present structured summary (objective / work items / files / verification / risks). STOP and WAIT for Lead Architect approval before proceeding.

vikunja_access_protocol.rationale: Stage 1 Anomaly A5 -- Executor surfaces vary in MCP-tool exposure; REST is documented as functionally equivalent fallback
vikunja_access_protocol.intro: Stage operations that read or write Vikunja MAY use either Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools OR direct Representational State Transfer (REST) against the Vikunja API. Both paths are byte-equivalent at the Vikunja server. Preference: MCP when the Executor surface exposes mcp__vikunja__* tools; REST is the documented fallback when MCP is not surfaced.
vikunja_access_protocol.preferred: Tool prefixes: mcp__vikunja__list_projects, mcp__vikunja__create_project, mcp__vikunja__list_tasks, mcp__vikunja__create_task, mcp__vikunja__update_task, mcp__vikunja__add_task_comment, mcp__vikunja__list_labels, mcp__vikunja__add_label_to_task. Precondition: Vikunja v2.3.0 server running on http://localhost:3456 (start via Start-Process C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\vikunja\vikunja-v2.3.0-windows-4.0-amd64.exe; poll /api/v1/info before first call).
vikunja_access_protocol.fallback: Direct REST via Invoke-RestMethod. Authentication flow: $cred = Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\.vscode\mcp.json -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json $pass = $cred.mcpServers.vikunja.env.VIKUNJA_PASS # read-only; do NOT write back $body = @{ username='blarai'; password=$pass } | ConvertTo-Json $token = (Invoke-RestMethod -Uri 'http://localhost:3456/api/v1/login' -Method POST ` -Body $body -ContentType 'application/json').token $headers = @{ Authorization = "Bearer $token" } # Subsequent calls: GET/PUT/POST against /api/v1/projects, /api/v1/tasks, # /api/v1/labels, etc.
vikunja_access_protocol.credential_source: Single canonical source for Vikunja credentials: C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\.vscode\mcp.json key path mcpServers.vikunja.env.VIKUNJA_PASS. Both MCP path and REST path read this same value. The User-scoped VIKUNJA_PASS env var (persisted at Stage 0.0.5) is used by the MCP server process itself; for direct PowerShell scripts, read from the .vscode/mcp.json file as shown above.
vikunja_access_protocol.safety: Neither path satisfies Master Plan section D.1 enforcement -- D.1 is enforced by tools/_vikunja_client.py (created in Stage 2.1) for fleet RUNTIME tools, not for stage-execution operations. Stage operations using either path must still pass explicit project_id when the operation is project-scoped.

v2_updates.note: Additive to v1 work_items 2.1–2.10. Apply alongside.

item.name: Add v2-baseline tools to multi-project refactor list
item.description: v1 refactor list missed these v2 tools. They MUST also accept --project-root (target project path) and resolve project_id via the platform-side registry, NOT a hardcoded 1.

additional_tools.t: Resolves the next active sprint/task. Add `--blarai-root` flag (rename `--project-root` in devplatform) defaulting to env BLARAI_ROOT. **Path correction (R12):** lives under `tools/autonomy_budget/`, NOT `tools/fleet_ops/` — earlier v2 drafts referenced the wrong directory.
Builds peer-review diffs. Add `--project-root` and pass through to git invocations.
Schema-validated parallel-sprint authorization. Add `--project-root` so it reads the right active_tasks.yaml.
Add `-ProjectRoot` parameter; resolve project_id from registry.

**G-1 (R12).** Has hardcoded `[string]$RepoRoot = 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI'` (line 17). Add `-BlarAIRoot` parameter with same env-fallback signature as wake_launcher (item 2.5.v2). Replace internal `$RepoRoot` references with `$BlarAIRoot`. Default keeps current behavior.

**G-1/G-4 (R12).** Has hardcoded `[string]$RepoRoot = 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI'` (line 38). This script is invoked **outside** the scheduled-task harness by Co-Lead during DEC-14.5 APPROVE motions, so XML-injected `--project-root` from Stage 4.7 cannot save it. Add `-BlarAIRoot` parameter with env fallback; rename internal `$RepoRoot` to `$BlarAIRoot`.
**G-1 (R12).** Has hardcoded `[string]$RepoRoot = 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI'` (line 32). Add `-BlarAIRoot` parameter; rename internal `$RepoRoot` to `$BlarAIRoot`.
**G-1 (R12).** Has hardcoded `[string]$RepoRoot = 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI'` (line 14). Add `-BlarAIRoot` parameter; rename internal `$RepoRoot` to `$BlarAIRoot`.

item.name: wake_launcher.ps1 needs explicit -BlarAIRoot parameter
item.description: wake_launcher.ps1 grew by ~655 LOC since v1. Needs explicit `-BlarAIRoot` parameter (NOT inferred from script location) so devplatform can launch wakes against an arbitrary target. Default to env BLARAI_ROOT, then C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI. **Correct path: `tools\scheduled-tasks\wake_launcher.ps1`** (NOT `tools\fleet_ops\` — the file has never lived there; earlier drafts mis-referenced it).

item.name: _vikunja_client must reject project_id spoofing in fleet_reports paths
item.description: Fleet Reports project (id=8) is special-purpose. Add an assertion in any fleet_reports helper that project_id matches registry.fleet_reports specifically — prevents accidental cross-project posting.

stage_prompt.stage_importance:
**This is the single highest-leverage stage.** It introduces the multi-project seam BEFORE anything physically moves, so Stages 3–5 become simple copies/deletes rather than refactors. It also bakes in Master Plan §D.1 (the Vikunja `project_id` hard rule) at the code level.
**All changes happen in the CURRENT BlarAI `tools/` tree.** Defaults preserve current behavior — the fleet still runs against BlarAI with no extra arguments — so this stage is fully reversible by `git revert`.
stage_prompt.objective:
Add three seams to every platform-layer tool living in `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\`: 1. Accept an explicit `--project-root` CLI argument (default: auto-detect from env/cwd/registry). 2. Accept an explicit `--project-id` (Vikunja project ID) CLI argument. 3. Route all Vikunja queries through a single `_vikunja_client.py` wrapper that enforces `project_id` is passed.
Defaults must preserve today's behavior: running `python -m tools.fleet_observability.daily_digest` with no arguments should still produce the same output as today.
stage_prompt.scope:
Tools to refactor (all under `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\`): - `fleet_observability/` (daily_digest, weekly_summary, dashboards) - `autonomy_budget/` (config loader, budget checker) - `gate_stale_cleaner/` - `fleet_ops/` (wake launchers, merge approvers) - `vikunja_mcp/` (bridge daemon, CLI) - `scheduled-tasks/` (PowerShell scripts — accept `-ProjectRoot` param)
NOT in scope: - `tools/vikunja/` (binary server — config change only in later stage) - `tools/openvino_contrib_agent/` (stays in BlarAI permanently) - BlarAI `services/`, `shared/`, `launcher/` — untouched

item.name: Create the _vikunja_client.py hard-rule wrapper
item.description: Single chokepoint for all Vikunja access. Refuses any list/search query without explicit int `project_id`.

item.name: Create project-root resolution helper
item.file:
"""Resolve the active project root and Vikunja project_id.
Resolution order: 1. Explicit cli_root / cli_project_id arguments (highest priority). 2. BLARAI_PROJECT_ROOT env var. 3. Walk up from cwd looking for a `.platform/vikunja_project_ids.yaml` file. 4. Walk up from cwd looking for a `.git` directory; pair with registry lookup. 5. Fallback: hard-coded BlarAI default (warns). """ from __future__ import annotations import os import warnings from dataclasses import dataclass from pathlib import Path
try: import yaml # PyYAML is already a dependency except ImportError as exc: # pragma: no cover - configuration error raise RuntimeError("PyYAML is required for _project_context") from exc
DEFAULT_BLARAI_ROOT = Path(r"C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI") REGISTRY_RELATIVE = Path(".platform/vikunja_project_ids.yaml")
@dataclass(frozen=True) class ProjectContext: root: Path vikunja_project_id: int name: str
def _load_registry(root: Path) -> dict: registry = root / REGISTRY_RELATIVE if not registry.is_file(): raise FileNotFoundError( f"Project registry not found at {registry}. " "Complete Stage 1.4 (registry seeding) first." ) with registry.open(encoding="utf-8") as fh: data = yaml.safe_load(fh) or {} return data
def _walk_up_for(name: Path) -> Path | None: cur = Path.cwd().resolve() for candidate in [cur, *cur.parents]: if (candidate / name).exists(): return candidate return None
def resolve( cli_root: str | os.PathLike | None = None, cli_project_id: int | None = None, ) -> ProjectContext: # 1. Explicit CLI override. if cli_root is not None: root = Path(cli_root).resolve() # 2. Env var. elif os.environ.get("BLARAI_PROJECT_ROOT"): root = Path(os.environ["BLARAI_PROJECT_ROOT"]).resolve() # 3. Walk up looking for the registry file directly. elif (found := _walk_up_for(REGISTRY_RELATIVE)): root = found.resolve() # 4. Walk up for .git as a weaker hint. elif (found := _walk_up_for(Path(".git"))): root = found.resolve() # 5. Fallback. else: warnings.warn( "Falling back to default BlarAI root; pass --project-root explicitly.", stacklevel=2, ) root = DEFAULT_BLARAI_ROOT
registry = _load_registry(root) name = registry.get("name") or root.name # Schema convention: each project's reverse-lookup yaml has a # "_project_id" key (e.g., "blarai_project_id" for the # project rooted at C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI). The generic "project_id" # key remains as a backwards-compat fallback for projects that opt # out of the prefixed convention. name_key = f"{root.name.lower()}_project_id" project_id = ( cli_project_id if cli_project_id is not None else registry.get(name_key) or registry.get("project_id") ) if project_id is None or not isinstance(project_id, int) or isinstance(project_id, bool): raise ValueError( f"Vikunja project_id missing or invalid in registry at {root}: {project_id!r}" ) return ProjectContext(root=root, vikunja_project_id=project_id, name=name)

item.name: Refactor autonomy_budget/config.yaml for per-project allowlist
item.description: Remove the hardcoded `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\` allowlist entries. Replace with a loader that reads `projects/<name>.yaml` based on the active ProjectContext.

item.name: Add --project-root / --project-id to every fleet CLI entrypoint
item.pattern: Every argparse parser gains: parser.add_argument("--project-root", default=None) parser.add_argument("--project-id", type=int, default=None) Entry code calls _project_context.resolve(cli_root, cli_project_id) and uses the result everywhere a hardcoded BlarAI path appears.

item.name: Refactor PowerShell scheduled-task scripts
item.description: Every `.ps1` in tools/scheduled-tasks/ and tools/fleet_ops/ that hardcodes `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI` now accepts `-ProjectRoot` param with default to auto-detect or existing path.

item.name: Audit all Vikunja call sites — replace direct HTTP with VikunjaClient
item.description: grep the codebase for direct `requests.get`/`httpx.get` calls to the Vikunja API (port 3456 / "/api/v1/tasks"), replace each with a VikunjaClient call. Any `list_tasks` / `search_tasks` call site that can't supply project_id is a BUG — fix it (the caller should know which project it's operating in).

item.name: Run full test suite
item.expected: All new tests pass. No regressions.

item.name: Smoke-test one fleet tool end-to-end
item.expected: Runs to completion. Output is equivalent to pre-refactor behavior.

item.name: Commit
item.command: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI; git add tools/; git commit -m "Stage 2: add multi-project seam to fleet tools (pre-move refactor)"

item.name: Update STATUS.md
item.description: Mark Stage 2 COMPLETE. Record commit hash and number of files touched.

rollback.trigger: Tests fail, smoke test fails, or fleet tool behavior changes.

steps.step: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI; git reset --hard HEAD~1 (on `chore/platform-extraction` branch)
OR: git revert HEAD if the commit has already been pushed (don't push this branch yet — keep local).
Fleet stays disabled from Stage 0 — no runtime impact.

exit_criteria.criterion: All fleet CLI entrypoints accept `--project-root` and `--project-id`.
No direct HTTP calls to Vikunja outside `_vikunja_client.py` (verified via grep).
Full test suite passes in BlarAI.
Smoke test of daily_digest produces equivalent output to pre-refactor.
